terms.definition: An immutable Git object holding a tree (file snapshot), parent pointer(s), author/committer identity + timestamp, and a message; identified by its content-addressed SHA hash.
A movable named pointer to a commit; physically a small file under `.git/refs/heads/` containing the commit's SHA.
The symbolic ref pointing at the currently checked-out commit, usually via a branch name.
The files on disk in the repo directory that you can edit; changes here are not tracked until staged.
The next-commit-to-be snapshot, populated by `git add`; sits between the working tree and the local repository.
The `.git/` directory holding all objects (commits, trees, blobs), refs (branches, tags) and config; what makes a folder a Git repo.
A named reference to another repository, typically hosted on GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket; `origin` by convention.
State where HEAD points directly at a commit instead of a branch; commits made here aren't reachable from any branch unless you create one.
Per-repo log of every change to HEAD (and to each branch); the safety net that lets you recover 'lost' commits after a bad rebase or branch delete.
